import React,{createContext,useContext,useEffect,useState}from"react";import{jsx as _jsx}from"react/jsx-runtime";const PreloadContext=/*#__PURE__*/createContext(undefined);export const PreloadProvider=_ref=>{let{children}=_ref;const[user,setUser]=useState(null);const[corporations,setCorporations]=useState([]);const[inventory,setInventory]=useState([]);const refresh=async()=>{try{const userRes=await fetch("/api/user").then(res=>res.json());setUser(userRes.user);const corpRes=await fetch("/api/corporations").then(res=>res.json());setCorporations(corpRes.corporations);// userRes.user is guaranteed at this point
const invRes=await fetch(`/api/inventory/${userRes.user.id}`).then(res=>res.json());setInventory(invRes.items||[]);console.log("Inventory preloaded",invRes.items);}catch(err){console.error("Error loading preload data",err);}};useEffect(()=>{refresh();},[]);return/*#__PURE__*/_jsx(PreloadContext.Provider,{value:{user,corporations,inventory,refresh},children:children});};export const usePreload=()=>{const ctx=useContext(PreloadContext);if(!ctx)throw new Error("usePreload must be used within a PreloadProvider");return ctx;};
